subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: A probability student is always late to class and arrives within ten minutes after the start of the class. Let $X$ be the time that elapses between the start of the class and the time the student arrives to class with a probability density function $f(x) = \begin{cases} kx^2 & 0 \le x \le 10 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ where $k > 0$ is a constant. Compute the value of $k$ and then find the probability that the student arrives less than 3 minutes after the start of the class.
